task_completion.description: The Multiplanar Reconstruction (MPR) viewer is opened with the loaded CT series.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The medical imaging software is open, displaying the Multiplanar Reconstruction (MPR) view (typically showing axial, coronal, and sagittal planes) with the CT series loaded.

task_completion.description: The projection mode is set to Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A UI element, button, or dropdown in the viewer clearly indicates that 'MIP' or 'Maximum Intensity Projection' is the active rendering mode.

task_completion.description: The slab thickness is configured to at least 15mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A UI element, text box, or slider in the viewer's settings shows the slab thickness value is set to 15 or greater.

task_completion.description: A full-screen screenshot of the configuration is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command, file manager window, screenshot tool, or notification shows a screenshot being saved to '~/DICOM/exports/mip_slab_view.png' or '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/mip_slab_view.png'.

task_completion.description: The applied parameters are documented in the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the creation or content of '~/DICOM/exports/mpr_settings.txt' or '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/mpr_settings.txt', containing the applied parameters (e.g., MIP, 15mm).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the medical imaging software to configure the MPR viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the software's menus and panels to set MIP and thickness, rather than mocking the configuration via terminal.

integrity.description: The agent captured a genuine screenshot of the configured GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of a screenshot tool being used to capture the screen, rather than copying or downloading a pre-existing image.

integrity.description: The settings documented in the text file match the actual configuration applied in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values written to the text file (MIP, thickness >= 15) correspond exactly to what was configured in the viewer's UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is /home/ga/DICOM/exports/mip_slab_view.png. The expected text file path is /home/ga/DICOM/exports/mpr_settings.txt. The minimum expected MIP slab thickness is 15.
